title: Jay Moseley — IPL and Customize MVS 3.8j (March 2025)

sections.title: Prerequisites & File Structure

added_this_step.purpose: Language compilers and tools (ALGOL, COBOL, FORTRAN, RPG, etc.)
Miscellaneous user datasets and VSAM clusters

host_files.purpose: REXX script to extract/display condition codes for jobs
Bash script using dasdinit to create empty DASD volumes
Bash script passing jobstream to socket reader via ncat
Hercules configuration file for the generated MVS system
Hercules command script to attach new DASD
Jobstreams for DASD init, catalogs, TSO users, TSO apps
Tape with RPF, QUEUE, REVIEW, DATE install datasets

macos_notes: brew install x3270 — installs c3270 terminal emulator
brew install nmap — installs ncat for job submission
Add 'shcmdopt enable' to Hercules config for host shell commands
Use 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost if connection fails

sections.title: SYSCPK — Language Compilers & Tools Volume
sections.description: A pre-built 3350 DASD volume containing language compilers and batch utilities. Required for IPL — SYSC.PROCLIB and SYSC.LINKLIB are integrated into the generated system.
sections.warning: MVS will NOT IPL without this volume present and ready

sections.title: Creating Additional DASD Volumes
notes: Volumes are raw — no VTOC or control structures until initialized by MVS
Compressed vs uncompressed: minimal difference on modern hardware
After creation, volumes must be attached to Hercules and initialized by ICKDSF

sections.title: IPL the MVS System (First Time)
steps: Start Hercules from the mvs directory
Start a TN3270 client and connect to localhost:3270 (must be TN3270, not telnet)
Console should connect to device 010 with LU name 'console'
In Hercules console: ipl 150 (loads from MVSRES volume)
MVS console shows IEA101A — system parameters prompt
First IPL: reply r 0,clpa (rebuilds Link Pack Area in paging files)
Subsequent IPLs: just press ENTER to accept defaults
Reply r 0,u to IFB010D (IPL reason code — not important for emulator)
Reply r 1,format,noreq to $HASP426 (format JES2 spool queues — first time only)
Reply r 2,y to $HASP479 (checkpoint dataset never formatted — first time only)
Reply r 3,y to $HASP436 (confirm checkpoint record change — first time only)
IPL complete when console settles — SETPFKEY and ZTIMER programs run from COMMND00
subsequent_ipl: Just press ENTER at IEA101A (no CLPA needed)

key_files.SYS1.PARMLIB(COMMND00): Console commands executed at IPL (starts SETPFKEY, ZTIMER)
key_files.SYS1.PARMLIB(SETPFK00): PF key definitions for MVS console
key_files.mvslog.txt: Hardcopy console output (all console messages captured here)

pfkey_commands.note: Use D PFK command to display current PF key settings

sections.ztimer: Runs hourly, purges JES2 output older than 5 days. Config in SYS2.CONTROL(ZTIMER).

socket_reader.protocol: Socket reader monitoring port 3505 for ASCII JCL input

ebcdic_reader.purpose: Reading card image files in EBCDIC format — for binary file transfer into MVS, NOT for job submission

sections.title: Initialize & Mount New DASD Volumes
steps: Attach volumes to Hercules: script conf/mvs.script
Submit job: sh ./submit.sh jcl/mvs00.jcl
ICKDSF prompts for confirmation — reply r <id>,U for each volume
Job submits a second MVS00 to vary volumes online and set mount attributes
Also imports SYSCPK User Catalog and defines SYSC aliases in Master Catalog
sections.post_step: Edit mvs.cnf — uncomment addresses 180 and 190 before next IPL

sections.title: JES2 Job Classes & Initiators
sections.description: JES2 controls job entry (readers), execution (initiators/classes), and output (printers/punches).
sections.class_s_note: System Programming class — embedded console commands execute without operator approval. Do NOT use for regular jobs.

punches.purpose: Binary file transfer from MVS to host (not JES2)

sections.spool_warning: Regularly purge held output to prevent spool filling up. ZTIMER auto-purges output older than 5 days.

sections.title: VSAM Catalogs & Dataset Organization

master_catalog.name: SYS1.VSAM.MASTER.CATALOG
master_catalog.warning: Finite capacity — avoid cataloging non-system datasets here

sections.password_prompt: After MVS01 sets Master Catalog password, all catalog updates require operator reply with password 'sysprog'

sections.title: Adding TSO User IDs
sections.catalog_password_replies: Reply r <id>,sysprog for each Master Catalog password prompt (Alias creation)

sections.title: TSO Applications — RPF, QUEUE, REVIEW, DATE

applications.name: RPF
applications.description: Rob Prins' Programming Facility — ISPF-like editor with dataset management, utilities, and foreground compile. The primary interactive editor.
applications.author: Rob Prins

applications.name: QUEUE
applications.description: View and control JES2 queues — held output, job status, purge jobs. The essential spool browser.
applications.author: Greg Price

commands.D: Display SYSOUT DDs for a job

applications.name: REVIEW
applications.description: Full screen browser and editor by Greg Price. Companion to QUEUE. Some tasks are easier in REVIEW than RPF.
applications.author: Greg Price
applications.help: At TSO READY: HELP REVIEW or HELP RFE

applications.name: DATE
applications.description: Displays system date (text and Julian format) and system time in multiple formats.
applications.author: Jay Moseley (rewrite)

sections.title: Starting VTAM & TSO
steps: Start a second TN3270 session — connects to device 400 with LU name 'tso'
On MVS console: s net (starts VTAM via NET cataloged procedure)
TSO starts automatically after VTAM initializes (via user modification)
IST025I messages about TCAM can be safely ignored
IKT007I indicates TSO is ready for logons
TSO terminal shows Network Solicitor screen
Type: logon hmvs01 (or your userid)
Enter password when prompted
Welcome message and TSO READY prompt appear
sections.note: More than 8 sessions requires editing VTAM and TSO parameters — advanced topic

sections.title: COBOL Compile, Link & Execute Workflow

steps.detail: At TSO READY prompt, type RPF and press Enter
Option 2 (EDIT) → Data set name: SOURCE → Cmd: s hello
steps.source:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID. HELLO.
ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
DATA DIVISION.
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
    DISPLAY 'HELLO FROM MVS 3.8J'
    STOP RUN.
steps.save: Type 'save' in Cmd field, then F3 twice

steps.detail: Option 2 (EDIT) → Member: cobucg, Data set: cntl → Use COLS command for ruler
Type 'submit' in Cmd field → IKJ56250I JOB HELLO(JOBnnnnn) SUBMITTED
steps.post: Type 'save', then F3 four times to exit RPF

steps.detail: In QUEUE, type 'd' in front of HELLO to see SYSOUT DDs. Type 's' in front of GO SYSOUT to see program output.
steps.troubleshooting: If no output: use 's' instead of 'd' to see JES2 JOB LOG, JCL listing, and messages. Use 'n' and 'p' to navigate output groups.

sections.cobucg_procedure: COBUCG = COBOL compile (COB), link edit (UC), and execute (G) in one cataloged procedure
sections.expected_messages: IEC130I for missing SYSPUNCH and SYSLIB DDs are informational — do not affect compilation

sections.title: Orderly Shutdown Procedure

steps.detail: Exit any running application back to TSO READY prompt
Log off all TSO sessions — terminal returns to Network Solicitor screen
Stop TSO — quick if all users logged off
Stop JES2 — may take a few seconds for managed processes to complete
End of day — writes final SMF records, switches SMF dataset
Flush all memory buffers to DASD — system enters wait state

warnings: Always shut down in this exact order to prevent DASD corruption
Never kill Hercules without QUIESCE — data loss risk
After shutdown, back up the dasd directory

sections.title: Hercules Hardware Configuration (mvs.cnf)

console.purpose: Alternate console (commented out — not needed for emulator)

card_readers.purpose: Socket reader on port 3505 — JES2 job submission
EBCDIC card reader — binary file transfer into MVS

card_punches.purpose: Binary file transfer from MVS to host

notes: Many more device addresses are generated into MVS than are attached in Hercules — this avoids needing a SYSGEN to add devices later
Do NOT comment out the hardcopy console at 015 — MVS will complain at IPL
Full hardware reference: mvs 3.8j generated hardware list.pdf
